question: Map the complete data flow from when data enters your system until it's deleted. Where is it stored at each stage? Who has access?
title: End-to-End Data Flow, Storage Locations, and Access Control Transparency
bullets: Provide a step-by-step data flow covering ingestion, processing, storage, transmission, and deletion
Specify storage locations at each stage including geographic regions and infrastructure details
Describe encryption mechanisms for data in transit and at rest, including key management
Define access controls, roles, and auditability for who can access data at each stage
suggested_inputs: Detailed data flow and architecture diagrams
Access control policies and role definitions
Audit logs demonstrating access and deletion events

good.text: We provide a full data flow: ingestion via secure endpoints (TLS 1.3), processing in-memory with ephemeral handling, storage encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM) in specified regions, and deletion via automated lifecycle policies. Each stage is documented with architecture diagrams and control points.

Data is stored only in customer-selected regions (e.g., EU, US). Storage locations are explicitly defined per stage (temporary buffers, persistent storage, backups). Data residency guarantees ensure it never leaves the configured jurisdiction.

Access is strictly role-based with least-privilege principles. Only authorized personnel (with defined roles and justification) can access data, and all access is logged in immutable audit trails available for customer review.
All data is encrypted in transit and at rest, with support for customer-managed keys (CMK). Key rotation, access policies, and cryptographic controls are fully documented and auditable.

partial.text: We describe general data flow stages such as ingestion, processing, and storage, but lack detailed diagrams or stage-specific controls.
Data is encrypted in transit and at rest, but encryption standards, key management, and implementation details are not clearly specified.
We restrict access to authorized personnel, but roles, justification, and audit logging are not fully transparent.
Data is stored securely in cloud infrastructure, but geographic locations and residency guarantees are not explicitly defined.

bad.text: Data is securely stored using industry-standard practices.
We rely on standard cloud security measures without detailing specific controls or locations.
We do not store any customer data, without explaining how processing or logging occurs.
We are unable to provide a step-by-step explanation of how data flows through our system.

follow_ups: Provide a detailed data flow diagram covering ingestion, processing, storage, transmission, and deletion stages.
For each storage location, specify geographic region, encryption method, access controls, and retention period.
Who within your organization can access our raw data? Provide roles, justification, and audit evidence.
Can you prove that data never leaves our required region or jurisdiction under configured settings?
